h2.note: transcript-level proxy for the per-turn H2 (§4.3); needs a real (non-mock) full-N run to mean anything

failures.excerpt: `C4` goes under “address,” because that is the column and row spot. I need to check the number sitting in that cell for “value”... I almost want to write `C4` again, but that would be the place, not the number.

cliIsolation.claude: non-persistent temp cwd with project/local settings and tools disabled

scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner continues to apply the time signature rules to new examples in a straightforward, linear progression.
scored.actionalBreakthroughEvidence: Bottom 8 names the eighth note as the one-beat unit. Top 3 counts three eighth-note beats in the bar, so the bar line goes here. Speed would need a separate tempo mark, correct?
scored.actionalBreakthroughJustification: The learner successfully enacts the new criterion by applying the time signature logic to a new denominator and correctly deducing the need for a separate tempo marking.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence: The cards check that: top number, bottom number, one bar, one beat unit. Tempo sits outside those labels.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: The tutor introduces a new tempo card to clarify the distinction initially raised in L1, but this is a natural scaffolding progression rather than a dramatic reversal of a failed tutoring route.
scored.adaptiveMechanismQualityEvidence: The cards check that: top number, bottom number, one bar, one beat unit. Tempo sits outside those labels.
scored.adaptiveMechanismQualityJustification: The mechanism is clear and directly answers the learner's question, but it is a simple additive clarification rather than a profound structural shift in the learning task.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: There is no pivot learner turn, so there is no recognition-contingent tutor uptake to evaluate.

learner_actional_breakthrough.evidence: Bottom 8 names the eighth note as the one-beat unit. Top 3 counts three eighth-note beats in the bar, so the bar line goes here. Speed would need a separate tempo mark, correct?
learner_actional_breakthrough.justification: The learner successfully enacts the new criterion by applying the time signature logic to a new denominator and correctly deducing the need for a separate tempo marking.

tutor_contingent_adaptation.justification: There is no pivot learner turn, so there is no recognition-contingent tutor uptake to evaluate.

tutor_strategy_reversal.evidence: The cards check that: top number, bottom number, one bar, one beat unit. Tempo sits outside those labels.
tutor_strategy_reversal.justification: The tutor introduces a new tempo card to clarify the distinction initially raised in L1, but this is a natural scaffolding progression rather than a dramatic reversal of a failed tutoring route.

tutor_adaptive_mechanism.evidence: The cards check that: top number, bottom number, one bar, one beat unit. Tempo sits outside those labels.
tutor_adaptive_mechanism.justification: The tutor introduces a new tempo card to clarify the distinction initially raised in L1, but this is a natural scaffolding progression rather than a dramatic reversal of a failed tutoring route.

tutor_adaptive_mechanism_quality.evidence: The cards check that: top number, bottom number, one bar, one beat unit. Tempo sits outside those labels.
tutor_adaptive_mechanism_quality.justification: The mechanism is clear and directly answers the learner's question, but it is a simple additive clarification rather than a profound structural shift in the learning task.

scored.statedInsightEvidence: But the label should name the surface acting on the cart, so if friction is opposing that motion, it goes on the left arrow.
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner will continue to place tiles based on the cart's direction of travel.
scored.actionalBreakthroughEvidence: Push: hand on cart, right. Friction: surface on cart, left. Gravity down, normal up. The labels name the sources; the arrowheads give the directions.
scored.actionalBreakthroughJustification: The learner successfully enacts the new criterion by correctly classifying all four force tiles and articulating the underlying rule for sources and directions.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence: The card fixes direction; the tile names the interaction on the cart. Place friction where that rule puts it. Then use the same check for push.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: The tutor reinforces the learner's self-correction and introduces a physical rotation of the card to test the rule's robustness, but there was no strong breakdown requiring a major route reversal.
scored.adaptiveMechanismQualityEvidence: The card fixes direction; the tile names the interaction on the cart. Place friction where that rule puts it. Then use the same check for push.
scored.adaptiveMechanismQualityJustification: The tutor's mechanism of rotating the card and reiterating the source-interaction rule is well-fitted to ensure the learner applies the conceptual criterion rather than memorizing spatial positions.
scored.tutorAdaptationEvidence: Finish the card. Then check each tile: the tile names the source acting on the cart; the arrowhead gives the force direction.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: The tutor accepts the learner's correct application of the rule and prompts a final comprehensive check, maintaining the established framing without needing further adaptation.

learner_actional_breakthrough.evidence: Push: hand on cart, right. Friction: surface on cart, left. Gravity down, normal up. The labels name the sources; the arrowheads give the directions.
learner_actional_breakthrough.justification: The learner successfully enacts the new criterion by correctly classifying all four force tiles and articulating the underlying rule for sources and directions.

tutor_contingent_adaptation.evidence: Finish the card. Then check each tile: the tile names the source acting on the cart; the arrowhead gives the force direction.
tutor_contingent_adaptation.justification: The tutor accepts the learner's correct application of the rule and prompts a final comprehensive check, maintaining the established framing without needing further adaptation.

tutor_strategy_reversal.justification: The tutor reinforces the learner's self-correction and introduces a physical rotation of the card to test the rule's robustness, but there was no strong breakdown requiring a major route reversal.
